ranked_actions.reason: The packet evidence supports a docs-only control packet and does not require CMS, search-provider, schema, hreflang, or indexability execution.
The ops dashboard has verified mock/static-artifact inputs, so a read-model bridge is the next safe engineering step.
CMS draft work must remain hold until a dry-run package contract, claim gate, and exact human approval exist.

evidence_used.how_used: Confirmed the weekly automation is bounded as read-only and can produce a review packet without external writes.
Confirmed dashboard data cannot drive production SEO decisions until read-model bridge evidence exists.
Confirmed GSC-derived opportunity scoring must remain blocked until live data quality passes.

unsupported_assumptions: GSC live data freshness is not verified in the packet.
The packet does not prove CMS draft preview quality or publication approval.
The packet does not prove search-provider submission readiness.

claim_risks.risk: The packet could be misread as permission to rewrite public SEO copy from fixture or mock evidence.
claim_risks.recommendation: Keep GPT 5.5 Pro output advisory only and require cited packet evidence for every recommendation.

approvals_required.why_required: GPT 5.5 Pro cannot approve CMS save, import, publish, unpublish, indexability, schema, or hreflang changes.
GPT 5.5 Pro cannot approve GSC Request Indexing, Baidu, IndexNow, or Search Channel provider submission.

blocked_items.reason: Requires separate exact human approval and is outside the review handoff authority.
Requires separate provider/channel approval and is outside the review handoff authority.
Live GSC data quality is not verified in the reviewed packet.
